[[Ryan Matterson]] (nephew) }} }} '''Terry Matterson''' (born 4 March 1967) is an Australian [[rugby league]] coach and former professional rugby league footballer. Matterson played in Australia's premiership for the [[Eastern Suburbs Roosters]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]], and was also selected to play in the [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] once in 1989. He was a [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|lk}} and he played the majority of his club football for the Brisbane Broncos, with whom he won the [[1992 NSWRL season|1992]] and [[1993 NSWRL season|1993]] premierships, in addition to stints with the [[Sydney Roosters|Eastern Suburbs Roosters]] and the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]. He worked as a head coach in the [[Super League]] for the [[Castleford Tigers]] between 2005 and 2011.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/coachprofile.php?p=coach&id=1|title=Coach Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727174039/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/coachprofile.php?p=coach&id=1|archive-date=27 July 2011}} From October 2013 Matterson was the head coach of the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]]. ==Background== Matterson was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales]], Australia.{{cn|date=March 2024}} ==Playing== After playing at {{rlp|fe}} for the [[Eastern Suburbs Roosters]], Matterson moved to Brisbane to be part of the Broncos' inaugural 1988 team. Playing at {{rlp|lk}} and being the team's goal-kicker, Matterson scored 24 points in the Broncos' first ever match in the opening round of [[brisbane Broncos 1988|1988]] against the previous year's premiers, the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. This would stand as the record for most individual points in a Broncos game for over fourteen years. In 1989 Matterson represented New South Wales in the [[Rugby League State of Origin|State of Origin]], playing against many of his Broncos' teammates. He was also named player of the series for the [[1989 Panasonic Cup (rugby league)|1989 Panasonic Cup]] tournament. Matterson went on to play in the Broncos' inaugural grand final victory in [[Brisbane Broncos 1992|1992]]. In the weeks following the grand final he travelled with the Broncos to England, where he played at lock forward in the [[1992 World Club Challenge]] against British champions [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], putting in a man-of-the-match performance to help Brisbane become the first NSWRL club to win the match in Britain. Matterson helped Brisbane to their second consecutive premiership in [[Brisbane Broncos 1993|1993]], scoring a try in the Grand Final against St George. Matterson joined the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in 1995. At the time he was the Brisbane club's top point-scorer, as well as record-holder of most points scored in a single match.{{cn|date=March 2024}} During the 2007 season at the Brisbane Broncos' 20-year anniversary celebration, the club announced a list of the 20 best players to play for them to date which included Matterson.{{cite news|last=Dekroo|first=Karl|title=Still the king|work=[[The Courier-Mail]]|place=Australia|publisher=Queensland Newspapers|date=9 May 2007|url=http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,21701434-10389,00.html|access-date=8 December 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070512101607/http://www.news.com.au/couriermail/story/0,23739,21701434-10389,00.html|archive-date=12 May 2007}} Matterson also was inducted into the Brisbane Broncos official Hall of Fame.{{cite web|title=Broncos Hall of Fame|url=http://www.broncos.com.au/broncos-hall-of-fame|work=broncos.com.au|publisher=[[Brisbane Broncos]]|access-date=31 December 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140126143431/http://www.broncos.com.au/broncos-hall-of-fame|archive-date=26 January 2014}} ==Coaching== Terry Matterson coached English [[Super League]] club the [[Castleford Tigers]] from 2005. Matterson expressed interest in taking over from [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]] as coach of the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at the end of the [[2008 National Rugby League season]].[https://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23185635-23214,00.html] Matterson stayed with the Castleford Tigers after they were relegated in 2006. He guided the team straight back up to regain its place in the [[Super League]]. Matterson then signed a new 3-year deal to stay at the Castleford Tigers. Matterson guided the Castleford Tigers to 7th place in Super League 2009. The club's first play-off finish since 2002. They faced Wigan Warriors at the DW Stadium and lost 18–12 with [[Joe Westerman]] scoring 8 points. Pressure grew on Matterson during 2010 after the Castleford Tigers lost to [[Barrow Raiders]] at home in the Challenge Cup. Several hundred supporters gathered at the back of the main stand and vented frustration at Matterson and the club's board.{{cn|date=March 2024}} Matterson and the team went on a rough patch but turned it around and only just missed out on a second play-off appearance in two years by losing out to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the final league game of the season. Matterson hoped to improve the Castleford Tigers further in 2011 which would be his last season with the club. In 2011, Matterson was named as the assistant coach of the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]], a team made up of Australian and New Zealand players contracted to [[Super League]] clubs. The Exiles played against the [[England national rugby league team|England]] in June 2011 in the [[Rugby League International Origin Match]], a fixture that is intended to become an annual contest in the future. Matterson will be assisting the Head coach of the RL Exiles, [[Brian McClennan]]. Matterson announced on 4 July 2011 that it would be his last season at the Castleford Tigers and he would leave the club at the end of that year. This took him to 6 years at the Castleford Tigers. Matterson will go back home to Australia to become assistant coach of the North Queensland Cowboys for 2012.{{cite web|url=http://www.code13rugbyleague.com/2011/09/20/terry-matterson-takes-nrl-role/|title=Terry Matterson takes NRL role|publisher=Code 13 Rugby League|date=2011-09-20|access-date=2011-09-20}} For their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign, the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] appointed Matterson as coach for the tournament. == Career stats == === NRL === {| Project|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2019-05-25}} ==Personal life== Matterson has a son named Charlie Matterson.{{cn|date=March 2024}} In January 2010, Matterson lost his left ring finger in a training accident where his wedding ring snagged on a fence. Surgery to re-attach the finger was unsuccessful, however Matterson seemed unaffected by the trauma, continuing to coach the Castleford Tigers the next week.{{cite news| url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/matterson-vows-to-continue-after-loss-of-finger-1872019.html| work=The Independent| location=London| title=Matterson vows to continue after loss of finger| first=Dave| last=Hadfield| date=19 January 2010| access-date=25 May 2010}} His older brother [[Greg Matterson]] became the head coach of [[NSW Cup]] side [[Newtown Jets]].{{cite web|url=http://www.thisischeshire.co.uk/sport/4239516.Antony_Jerram_milestone_in_Australian_rugby_league/|title=Former Warrington Wolves back rower Antony Jerram reaches milestone in Australian rugby league|publisher=This is Cheshire|date=2009-03-29|access-date=2010-02-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110113150118/http://www.thisischeshire.co.uk/sport/4239516.Antony_Jerram_milestone_in_Australian_rugby_league/|archive-date=13
